reasoning: As of late April 2025, Libya has not yet ratified the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) Agreement. Libya has been listed continuously over the last few years as one of a small handful of countries that signed the agreement but have yet to deposit their instrument of ratification. Currently, Libya is deeply divided politically between rival governments which makes the ratification of international treaties by its parliament (the House of Representatives) highly difficult and generally not a priority. There are also no current news reports in either English or Arabic indicating any ongoing momentum or drafting in the Libyan legislature regarding the AfCFTA. With the resolution date (July 1, 2025) merely two months away from the knowledge cutoff, the timeline is exceedingly tight for any unexpected political breakthrough to result in swift ratification. Therefore, the probability of Libya ratifying before July 1, 2025 is extremely low.
